chapters.file-on-disk: File on disk, environment when mounted

events.text: Time to create a 2 gigabyte encrypted repository on the server. A repository is a single encrypted file at rest. Creating it also mounts it, so it immediately becomes a folder with its own Docker daemon and isolated application data, completely separate from everything else.
events.afterText: Repository created and already mounted on the server, ready to use.
events.prose: Create a 2 GB encrypted repository on the server. A repo is a single encrypted file at rest. Creating it mounts it right away: a folder with its own Docker daemon and isolated application data.

events.text: Let's confirm the new repo is registered. From here it can be deployed, forked, backed up, or opened in your editor, all from your laptop.
events.afterText: There it is, registered and ready to use.
events.prose: Confirm the new repo is registered. Once listed, it can be deployed, forked, backed up, or opened in an editor from your laptop.

events.text: One handy option: open the repo directly in VS Code with this command. We won't launch the editor here, but it starts a remote session into the repo over SSH so you can edit its files.
events.afterText: Run that on your laptop to edit the repo's files in VS Code.
events.prose: Open the repo directly in VS Code with rdc vscode connect. It starts a remote SSH session into the repo so you can edit its files in your editor.

narrations.text: Let's create your first repository! It's an encrypted file at rest that turns into a fully isolated environment the moment you create it.

On disk it's a single encrypted image. Once mounted, you get a dedicated Docker daemon, application data inside the encrypted volume, and loopback IPs that won't collide with anything else on the server.
That's your first repository, ready to go. Next up, we'll deploy an app inside it!
